name: Mitochondrial delivery and transplantation bootstrap for June 3, 2026
summary: Revised bootstrap bundle establishing the first public baseline candidate for the mitochondrial delivery and transplantation track.

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Mitochondrial Delivery and Transplantation, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse young-mitochondria mitotherapy source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source anchors the direct aged-animal functional signal for the track.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse hippocampal mitochondrial transfusion source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source adds target-tissue mitochondrial biomarker support in aged animals.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged rat prefrontal-cortex young-mitochondria source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supplies a second aged-animal CNS mechanistic signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse and elderly human CD4 T-cell mito-transfer source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supplies elderly-human ex vivo support while retaining the treated-human boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add pediatric cardiac mitochondrial transplantation pilot source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source captures the most efficacy-adjacent treated-human signal while preserving acute pediatric disease limits.

proposed_changes.summary: Add acute stroke autologous mitochondrial transplant phase 1 source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source adds human-brain safety and feasibility context without upgrading aging efficacy.

proposed_changes.summary: Add recruiting pediatric ECMO ischemia mitochondrial transplantation registry source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This registry is trial-watch context and should not be interpreted as posted efficacy evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add recruiting cerebral ischemia mitochondrial transplant registry source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This registry contextualizes the stroke phase 1 publication and confirms the no-results-posted boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize autologous mitochondrial transplantation.
proposed_changes.rationale: The intervention record makes the human procedural evidence inspectable while preserving source, route, and disease-context limits.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize young-donor mitochondrial transfusion.
proposed_changes.rationale: The intervention record groups the direct aged-animal mitotherapy evidence and distinguishes it from autologous human acute-disease transplantation.

proposed_changes.summary: Normalize ex vivo mitochondrial transfer.
proposed_changes.rationale: The intervention record keeps elderly-human cell evidence separate from treated-human evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse mitotherapy study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study anchors the animal_signal stage assignment.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse hippocampal mitochondrial transfusion study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study adds aged-animal mitochondrial biomarker support.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged rat young-mitochondria stress study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study adds a small mechanistic CNS aging-context signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse and elderly human CD4 T-cell mito-transfer study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study captures the human-cell support while preserving ex vivo directness limits.

proposed_changes.summary: Add pediatric cardiac autologous mitochondrial transplantation pilot study.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study records the strongest human clinical signal while keeping it disease-specific.

proposed_changes.summary: Add acute cerebral ischemia autologous mitochondrial transplant phase 1 study.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study records human-brain safety and feasibility context.

proposed_changes.summary: Add cardiac ECMO autologous mitochondrial transplantation trial-watch activity item.
proposed_changes.rationale: This trial status is relevant context but not efficacy evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add cerebral ischemia autologous mitochondrial transplantation trial-watch activity item.
proposed_changes.rationale: This trial status contextualizes the phase 1 source while retaining the no-registry-results boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse functional mitotherapy finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports the animal_signal stage assignment.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged mouse hippocampal SDHB biomarker finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports the animal biomarker portion of the baseline.

proposed_changes.summary: Add aged rat PFC apoptosis marker finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding adds a second aged-animal CNS mechanism signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add elderly human CD4 T-cell ex vivo mito-transfer finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports human-cell plausibility while limiting the stage because it is ex vivo.

proposed_changes.summary: Add pediatric acute cardiac mitochondrial transplantation pilot finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding records the human clinical signal and its acute pediatric boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add acute stroke phase 1 safety finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports clinical feasibility and safety context but not aging efficacy.

proposed_changes.summary: Create the first public outlook for the Mitochondrial Delivery and Transplantation track.
proposed_changes.rationale: A low-confidence animal_signal baseline is justified by aged-animal functional and biomarker findings, while human evidence remains acute disease-specific or ex vivo.
uncertainty_flags: The direct aging stage anchor is animal evidence.
The strongest human clinical signal is pediatric acute ischemia-reperfusion injury, not aging.
The acute stroke phase 1 result is safety and feasibility evidence only.
The elderly-human T-cell result is ex vivo and should not be treated as a human intervention trial.
Overlap with mitochondrial biogenesis/dynamics, mitophagy, cellular senescence, and immune aging requires review.

proposed_outlook_implications.note: A first public baseline is justified at low-confidence animal_signal because aged-animal mitochondrial delivery studies show functional or tissue-biomarker improvement, while human evidence remains acute disease-specific or ex vivo.
Publishing this bundle would broaden mitochondrial dysfunction coverage to procedural and cell-therapy delivery approaches while keeping the stage below treated-human aging benefit.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
